title: The Promise Comes Due
subtitle: An ageing nation and a shrinking pot.

turns.prompt: The actuaries are blunt: the national pension fund runs dry within a decade. Something has to give.

choices.label: Raise the retirement age
choices.reaction: The books improve. Older workers feel betrayed.

choices.label: Raise contributions on workers and firms
Borrow now to keep every promise
choices.flavour: Pay the pensions, push the problem.

delayed.headline: Markets begin to question whether the promises are payable

turns.prompt: The immediate panic eases. Now you can shape the long game.

choices.label: Invest the fund for higher returns

delayed.headline: A market downturn guts the pension pot overnight

choices.reaction: Returns look great. The risk stays hidden.

choices.label: Means-test benefits to target the needy
choices.reaction: Fairer in theory. The middle feels squeezed out.

choices.label: Invest in families and childcare
choices.reaction: A long bet on a bigger next generation.

turns.prompt: A wave of retirements hits, and younger workers march against shouldering the burden.

choices.label: Broker a deal between young and old
choices.reaction: A fragile pact. Nobody's thrilled; everybody signs.

choices.label: Cut benefits across the board
choices.reaction: The math works. The streets don't.

choices.label: Welcome young workers to widen the tax base
choices.reaction: The workforce grows. The politics get loud.

turns.prompt: The clever fixes from before come back wearing the faces of real people.

memory_callbacks.text: The market bet you made has blown a hole straight through the pension pot.
The higher retirement age has become a rallying cry against you.
The benefit cuts have pushed pensioners into visible, photographed poverty.
The borrowing that kept the promises is now a debt the young refuse to carry.

choices.label: Pass a sustainable, fair pension settlement
choices.reaction: Built to last. The transition is brutal.

choices.label: Cover the gap with cheap money
choices.reaction: The cheques clear. The currency wobbles.

choices.label: Shift to private retirement accounts
choices.flavour: Hand the risk to each citizen.
choices.reaction: Freedom and risk, handed to everyone alike.

turns.prompt: The reckoning passes. How does your society grow old?

choices.label: A solidarity model: all pay in, all are covered
choices.reaction: A nation that grows old together. It isn't cheap.

choices.label: A lean state: low taxes, self-provision
choices.flavour: Light, cheap, and on your own.
choices.reaction: Light and efficient. The unlucky fall furthest.

choices.label: Outgrow the problem entirely
choices.flavour: If growth holds, all is well.
choices.reaction: If the growth holds, all is well. If.
